summary: The Athletic Trainer role is fundamentally incompatible with your priorities, as the 10–14 hour days and mandatory night and weekend commitments preclude any sense of predictable work-life balance. Beyond the extreme burnout risk and high-stakes legal liability, the job requires high-level analytical reasoning and quantitative skills that conflict with your preference for procedural, protocol-driven work. Consequently, this career path would likely lead to rapid exhaustion and professional dissatisfaction given your specific needs and cognitive strengths.
